cards.back: I would like to book a room.
cards.detail: A useful travel phrase. The agent should keep the phrase close to the source sentence instead of inventing a new one.
A noun card can keep article information attached when the target language needs it.
A verb card can later add structured tables when the domain skill needs challenge mode.

texts.title: A Short Hotel Dialogue
texts.body:
Bonjour, je voudrais reserver une chambre pour deux nuits.
Bien sur. Vous preferez une chambre simple ou double ?
Une chambre double, s'il vous plait.
texts.translation:
Hello, I would like to book a room for two nights.
Of course. Do you prefer a single or double room?
A double room, please.
